This recording was made in Rijsterbos, a 172-hectare (mostly) deciduous forest in Friesland (NL), planted in 1676 and dominated by mature oaks and beeches. It borders the IJsselmeer to the southwest and agricultural grasslands (mainly dairy farms) on other sides. The forest has varied undergrowth and offers diverse microhabitats. Human presence is moderate; visitors with dogs are common but dispersed. Occasional disturbance from nearby farm machinery along the forest edge may occur, though this is minimal.

Nocturnal migration. Recorded from backyard. Olympus LS12 with 2 Primo EM172 mics. An unintended recorder setting produced a lot of noise, therefore the recording was high pass filtered (1.5 kHz) using Audicity.

Recording not modified. Bird singing from a small tree in a sunken garden between my (underground) car park and apartment building. This makes the acoustic value of this recording great and on top of that there is little traffic in this area. Great concert on a warm summer evening.

loc: Camping Vinkenhof, Schin op Geul, Zuid-Limburg
rmk: Dawn chorus heavily dominated by Blackbirds, perched on lantern poles and topped leafless trees spread out among the open landscape. A cuckoo sings from the wooded slope to the west (Soesberg). Other singers include common woodland/garden birds such as Eurasian Wren, Dunnock and Great Tit. Recorded with internal microphone of Edirol R09-HR.

The blackbird was sitting in the top of a nearby tree. I could see him, he had black feathers, so it was definately a male. I was on my balcony, 10 meters away, on the same height als the bird in the tree.  The weather was very peacefull, no wind, sun  in a low angle without any clouds. Really enjoyed this bird's song. Recording is amplified with 10db.
